reasoning: The document contains multiple major defects that preclude automatic approval, most notably the failure to extract critical tabular data on slides 6 and 8 and the widespread rendering failure of maps and charts which are central to a hurricane coordination document. While programmatic shingling scores are high, the visual score of 0.5 and the low image count ratio (0.457) strongly corroborate the LLM's findings of broken imagery and structural loss.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The conversion failed to extract the text content from the tables on Slide 6 and Slide 8. On Slide 6, the 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' table contains approximately 23 rows of coordinate and location data, which constitutes the vast majority of the slide's information. Similarly, on Slide 8, the data table for 'Elevation Difference and Volume' was not extracted. This results in a loss of significantly more than 25% of the text content for these sections.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Slide 6 contains a detailed table of 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' with columns for Longitude, Latitude, Location, State, District, and Overwash info. Slide 8 contains a data table with columns Section, Volume Change, MHW Volume Change, Shoreline Change, etc.
concerns.output_quote: The text and data from these tables are entirely absent from Section 6 and Section 8 of the JSON output.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains several data tables, most notably on Page 6 (Ground Truth Locations) and Page 8 (Elevation Difference and Volume Change). These tables have clear row/column structures. However, the output fails to extract any of these as structured table objects (`type: 'table'`). Instead, the content is captured entirely within image elements, resulting in a total loss of the document's tabular structure.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Page 6: Table with columns 'Longitude', 'Latitude', 'Location', 'State'.
Page 8: Table with columns 'Section', 'Volume Change', 'Shoreline Change'.
concerns.output_quote: Section 6 and Section 8 only contain image and paragraph elements; no table elements are present.

concerns.analysis: Multiple meaningful images are broken or missing in the HTML rendering. Specifically, the maps and charts on pages 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14 are rendered as pixelated gray-and-white placeholders or solid green boxes. These images are central to the content of the presentation, showing survey areas, data production status, and bathymetric imagery. While the image on page 4 rendered correctly, the failure of the majority of other images is a significant defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Meaningful maps and charts on pages 5, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14 (e.g., '2016 Post-Matthew Operations Overview' map on page 5).
concerns.output_quote: Pixelated gray/white placeholders or solid green boxes in place of maps and charts throughout the document.

concerns.analysis: There are two instances of content duplication in the output. First, in Section 7, the title 'Post-Matthew Data Production Status Map' appears as the section title and is then repeated as a paragraph element in the content, whereas it only appears once on the corresponding source page (Page 7). Second, in Section 12, the text 'ENVI Relative Depth Image and Danger Area Boundary (in black)' is duplicated; it appears both as the caption for the second image and as a standalone paragraph immediately
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Page 7: 'Post-Matthew Data Production Status Map'
Page 13: 'ENVI Relative Depth Image and Danger Area Boundary (in black)'
concerns.output_quote:
Section 7: title field and element index 2.
Section 12: image 2 caption and element index 2.

action_items: Manually extract and format the data tables from slides 6 and 8 into accessible HTML tables
Remediate the broken map and chart images throughout the presentation
Remove duplicated text strings in sections 7 and 12
